rulings.Case Title: ANDREW PILLET VS. STEPHEN H KENDRICK ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answers To Third Set Of Form Interrogatories And Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday July 30, 2018, Line 1, PLAINTIFF ANDREW PILLET'S Motion To Compel Answers To Third Set Of Form Interrogatories And Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge James Fleming, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion to compel defendant Juniper Twelve, LLC to answer plaintiff's third set of form interrogatories under oath without objection is unopposed and shall be granted in its entirety, with full compliance due within ten days of the order hereon. Defendant Juniper Twelve, LLC shall further pay sanctions for discovery abuse to plaintiff in the amount of $1000.00 within ten days of the order hereon. Counsel to moving party shall submit a proposed order in Word format consistent with the foregoing by email to jameshfleming@gmail.com at least 24 hours prior to the scheduled hearing. If this Tentative Ruling is uncontested and said order is timely submitted, no appearance by moving party is required. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jameshfleming@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JUVENAL VIRRUETE ET AL VS. FORT 1, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For An Order Compelling Defendants' Depositions And Written Discovery Responses And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday July 30, 2018, Line 2, PLAINTIFFS JUVENAL VIRRUETE, JOAQUIN DELGADO, DANIEL GONZALES' Motion For An Order Compelling Defendants' Depositions And Written Discovery Responses And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge James Fleming, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion of plaintiffs for an order compelling defendants' depositions and written discovery responses is unopposed and shall be granted in its entirety, with full compliance due without objection within thirty days of the order hereon. Defendants and their counsel shall further pay, jointly and severally, sanctions for discovery abuse to plaintiffs in the amount of $2510.00, within ten days of the order hereon. Counsel to moving party shall submit a proposed order in Word format consistent with the foregoing by email to jameshfleming@gmail.com at least 24 hours prior to the scheduled hearing. If this Tentative Ruling is uncontested and said order is timely submitted, no appearance by moving party is required. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jameshfleming@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DEMAS YAN VS. BRYANT FU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION - Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Requiring Demas Yan To Post Security
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Monday, July 30, 2018, Line 7, PLAINTIFF DEMAS YAN'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Requiring Demas Yan To Post Security. Plaintiff Demas Yan's motion for reconsideration of the June 29, 2018 order requiring him to post security is denied. None of the authorities cited by Mr. Yan show that the motion filed by defendants Bryant Fu and Crystal Lei to require Mr. Yan to post security was incorrectly decided. Mr. Yan has not cited any California authority, nor is the court aware of any such authority, that the conduct allegedly committed by Mr. Fu and Ms. Lei is actionable in a civil case. Significantly, the complaint does not allege that either Mr. Fu or Ms. Lei knew that Tony Fu planned to commit an act of violence against Mr. Yan or that either of them was present when the act of violence occurred or took any step that facilitated Tony Fu in committing an act of violence against Mr. Yan. At most the complaint alleges that Mr. Fu and Ms. Lei participated in a cover up of an act of violence committed by Tony Fu so that Tony Fu could avoid criminal liability. No case or statute states that civil liability lies in these circumstances. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Fi and Ms. Lei is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ALBERT TINDLE VS. UNITED PARCEL SERVICE OF AMERICA, INC. A DELAWARE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Monday, July 30, 2018, Line 9, DEFENDANT UNITED PARCEL SERVICE, INC.'S (IDENTIFIED AS "UNITED PARCEL SERVICE OF AMERICA, INC.") DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant United Parcel Service, Inc's demurrer to all four causes of action alleged in the complaint filed by Albert Tindle is sustained without leave to amend as to the first cause of action for negligent hiring and sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the second, third, and fourth causes of action for assault, battery, intentional infliction of emotional distress. "Where the complaint affirmatively alleges facts indicating coverage by the workers' compensation laws, if it fails to state additional facts negating application of the exclusive remedy provision, no civil action will lie and the complaint is subject to a general demurrer." (Roberts v. Pup 'N' Taco Driveup (1984) 160 Cal.App.3d 278, 284). The negligent hiring claim is barred by the exclusivity rule and there is no reasonable possibility that Mr. Tindle can state a viable negligent hiring claim. Claims against an employer for negligent hiring predicated on an assault by a negligently hired co-employee are barred by the exclusivity rule. That rule applies to employer conduct except "intentional misconduct which goes beyond employer's failure to assure that the tools or substances used by the employee or the physical environment of a workplace are safe." (Cole v. Fair Oaks Fire Protection Dist. (1987) 43 Cal.3d 148, 158; Coit Drapery Cleaners, Inc. v. Sequoia Ins. Co. (1993) 14 Cal.App.4th 1595, 1606 ("any claim for mere negligence ¿ would be barred here by the workers' compensation laws ¿ [based on] negligent or improper supervision"). The exclusivity rule also bars Mr. Tindle from seeking recovery in tort from his employer for injuries sustained as a result of the physical attack committed against him by a co-employee during the scope and course of Mr. Tindle's employment. (Fretland v. County of Humboldt (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 1478, 1487). Mr. Tindle's allegation that UPS was or should have been aware of the co-employee's anger issues but nevertheless hired him, does not show UPS's "voluntary election" to adopt the assault by the co-employee as UPS's own conduct. Mr. Tindle's IIED claimed is also barred because it is not supported by facts showing intentional conduct going beyond the inherent risks of employment. (Cole (IIED claim based on reckless but not intentional conduct by the employer is precluded by the exclusivity rule)). If he can do so in good faith, Mr. Tindle is given leave to allege facts showing UPS ratified the intentionally tortious conduct of his co-employee and to allege intentional conduct by UPS beyond the normal risks of employment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for UPS is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
